pages.text: The Crafting Terminal combines item access with three workspaces: the $(thing)Storage tab$() for stored items, the $(thing)Craftable tab$() for recipes ready now, and the $(thing)Fuel tab$() for resources and stations.
See $(l:magic_storage:recipes)Recipe Catalog$() for the exact crafting layout.

Install the matching station on the Fuel tab, then select an output in Storage or Craftable. The preview shows the exact recipe, ingredients, station, process energy, Fuel, or Axe Energy needed. A neutral message appears when no supported recipe is available.

Use the shared ×1, ×8, ×64, or Max strip. Fixed amounts dim when the full batch cannot be made. Max checks ingredients, energy, and destination room together and chooses the largest complete amount that currently fits.

Craft Output defaults to Player: results fill your inventory first and overflow returns to the Core. Choose Storage to send the complete batch to the Core. If every result and container remainder cannot fit, nothing is consumed.

EMI is the required client recipe viewer and may request the exact recipe shown by the terminal, sending its result to the chosen cursor or inventory destination. This remains one-level magic crafting: missing intermediates are not crafted recursively.

Craftable may show an output with zero stored quantity when its recipe can run now. Such an entry selects a recipe but is not a stored stack to extract. See $(l:magic_storage:recipe_costs)Recipe Costs and Support$() for station and recipe limits.
